best_run_loop: Step onto Martin Goodman Trail at Bathurst Quay (3 min). East to Sugar Beach = 4km out-and-back; west to Ontario Place = 6km; Humber Bay Arch Bridge = 12km out-and-back. Bentway under Gardiner adds 1.75km traffic-free corridor.
running_insight: Lakefront access is the killer feature: 100km/week trivially doable since you can stack 10-20km Martin Goodman runs daily without crossing a road. Tribe Fitness is essentially your front door.

best_run_loop: South under rail corridor to Coronation Park (5 min) onto Martin Goodman Trail. West to Humber Bay Arch = 10-12km round trip; east to CityPlace = 6km. Liberty Village → Parkdale → High Park loop = 8km classic.
running_insight: Best of both worlds: in the Liberty Village run-club epicenter (Striders + Parkdale RR + F45 all <20 min) AND 5 min to the lake. King & Bathurst nightlife is a hike (20 min) — Uber it.

best_run_loop: Cross to Bremner → Martin Goodman Trail at HTO Park (5 min). East to Sugar Beach/Distillery = 5km; west to Ontario Place = 7km. Toronto Music Garden mid-route. Easy 10-15km lakefront daily.
running_insight: Sits between CityPlace and South Core with quick access to Barry's, Spinco, and waterfront. King W nightlife (King/Spadina) is essentially across the street.

best_run_loop: South to Coronation Park (6 min) → Martin Goodman Trail. Humber Bay out-and-back = 12km. Or High Park gates via Queen W = 3.5km one-way (perfect 10-12km loop with the park's 4km perimeter).
running_insight: Liberty Village is arguably Toronto's #1 zip code for serious recreational runners — you're 5 min from the lake AND in the run-club density bubble. Loblaws King & Shaw (9 min) is a second grocery option.

run_clubs.name: Parkdale Roadrunners
run_clubs.meets: Tue 7pm Stay Gold, Sat 9am Bather (450 Dufferin)

best_run_loop: South to Coronation Park → Martin Goodman Trail (6 min). Humber Bay loop = 12km; High Park via Queen W = ~10km loop. Liberty Village → Parkdale → Roncesvalles is a classic 8km social run.
running_insight: Same as the other Liberty Village picks — bullseye on East Liberty puts you on top of F45, Metro, and the LV Striders meetup. 100km/week is structurally easy.

run_clubs.name: Tribe Fitness
run_clubs.location: 10 Lower Spadina (Canoe Landing across the street)

best_run_loop: Onto Martin Goodman Trail via Bathurst Quay/HTO Park in 3-4 min. Daily lakefront: 5km east to Sugar Beach, 10-12km west to Humber Bay Arch. Bentway loop adds variety. Canoe Landing track on-site for intervals.
running_insight: Tribe Fitness is literally across Canoe Landing Park. For a 100km/week runner this is the most convenient address on the list — 5 min to lakefront AND to your run-club home base.

best_run_loop: Martin Goodman Trail access via Bathurst Quay in 4-5 min. Sugar Beach east = 4km; Humber Bay Arch west = 11km. Toronto Music Garden + Music Garden Promenade are gems on the route.
running_insight: CityPlace canonical address — lake-close, Tribe-close, Sobeys-close. Slightly further from Liberty Village run clubs but BlackToe King West fills that gap.

best_run_loop: Step onto the Bentway in 2 min (1.75km traffic-free under Gardiner). Drop south to Martin Goodman at Bathurst Quay (6 min) for 10-15km lake runs. Fort York Historic Site green space immediately adjacent.
running_insight: Fort York sits between Liberty Village and CityPlace — you can flex either way for run clubs. Bentway directly out the door is unique: traffic-free, lit, all-weather.

run_clubs.name: Parkdale Roadrunners
run_clubs.meets: Tue 7pm Stay Gold, Sat 9am Bather (450 Dufferin)

best_run_loop: Coronation Park → Martin Goodman in ~6 min. Humber Bay out-and-back = 12km. Easy access to Parkdale loops + High Park (3.5km away, then 4km perimeter = 11km loop total).
running_insight: Western-most Liberty Village = closest to Parkdale Roadrunners' Saturday meetup and High Park, but furthest from King W nightlife. For a 100km/week runner this trade is worth it; for socializing it isn't.

best_run_loop: Down to Queens Quay → Martin Goodman Trail in 5-7 min. East to Distillery District/Sugar Beach = 5km; west to CityPlace/Bathurst = 6km. Total Toronto Islands loop accessible by ferry from Jack Layton terminal (5 min walk).
running_insight: Best gym situation on the list (Equinox Bay is 12 min) but worst for run clubs — Liberty Village + Parkdale crews are 25-40 min away. Trade-off: financial district convenience but socially isolated from the run scene.

run_clubs.name: Parkdale Roadrunners
run_clubs.meets: Tue 7pm Stay Gold, Sat 9am Bather (450 Dufferin)

best_run_loop: Coronation Park to Martin Goodman in ~6 min. Humber Bay = 12km out-and-back. Parkdale streets + Roncesvalles for variety. High Park = 10km tempo loop.
running_insight: Same Liberty Village logic — incredible run-club density, gym next door, grocery next door. King W nightlife requires a 19-min walk or 5-min Uber.

best_run_loop: Down to Queens Quay → Martin Goodman in 4-5 min. East to Distillery = 5km; west to CityPlace = 6km. Easy 10km out-and-back along lake. Toronto Islands ferry 5 min away for off-road island runs (12km island loop).
running_insight: Best grocery (Longo's downstairs) and Equinox Bay 11 min, but you'd Uber to every run club. For a 100km/week runner, lakefront access is still fine — it's the social side that suffers.

best_run_loop: Cross to Bremner → Martin Goodman Trail (5-6 min). Sugar Beach east = 4km; Humber Bay west = 11km. Toronto Music Garden along the way. Bentway loop nearby for traffic-free intervals.
running_insight: Sweet spot between CityPlace and South Core. King W nightlife within 12 min, three boutique gyms within 12 min, lakefront in 7. Slightly inferior to Capreol on Tribe proximity but better on Barry's.

best_run_loop: Martin Goodman via Bathurst Quay in 3-4 min. Bentway under Gardiner is 2 min away (1.75km traffic-free corridor). Lake runs 5-15km daily; Fort York Historic Site green space attached.
running_insight: Effectively tied with 10 Capreol for best 100km/week base: Tribe is across the park, Sobeys is across the street, Bentway and Martin Goodman are in front of the building. The most 'frictionless' running address on the list.

run_clubs.name: Parkdale Roadrunners
run_clubs.meets: Tue 7pm Stay Gold (124 Brock), Sat 9am Bather (450 Dufferin)

best_run_loop: South under rail to Marilyn Bell Park / Sunnyside Beach (8 min) → Martin Goodman Trail. Humber Bay Arch = 8-10km round trip. High Park gates 2.5km north = 5km tempo + 4km park perimeter = great 12-14km long-run loop. Strava heatmap shows this corridor is the densest training stretch in west-end Toronto.
running_insight: On the doorstep of Parkdale Roadrunners' Saturday start line — the most serious-runner address on the list. But it's the worst for King W nightlife (34 min walk; Uber required). For a 100km/week + Sept exam grind, this is the 'focused training' pick; for socializing post-exam it's weaker.
